document_format_anchoring:NEW — unit 27 (outcome: Corrected) produced a long engineering-oriented model evaluation document when user wanted shorter sales/corporate-audience content; unit 28 (is_correction=true) was accepted after adjusting framing; this is a deliverable-scope calibration failure distinct from classic feature-scope-expansion; the agent did not anchor document format to the user's audience before writing
recommendations: ls-t-for-timestamp-sort is now the dominant violation subvariant (4 of 6 violated units) and is not covered by existing ls-related hooks; the pattern J=$(ls -t /tmp/prefix_*.json | head -1) for finding the most-recent temp file has no clean LS/Glob equivalent since those tools lack timestamp-sort; a targeted Bash snippet using find -newest or python3 -c 'import glob,os; print(max(glob.glob(...),key=os.path.getmtime))' should be established as the canonical substitute to displace the reflex

document_format_anchoring is new and intervention cost is low: one precheck before writing any deliverable document (eval report, summary) — confirm audience and length target with the user rather than inferring from the task description — would have prevented the unit-27 correction

grep_in_compound_bash absence across all units is a genuine improvement from three consecutive RECURRING cycles; if this holds in the next cycle, downgrade to monitoring-only and remove from active bias list
